subject: Physics
title: Newton's Prism Splits Light
description: A micro-lesson that makes Newton's Prism Splits Light usable.

hook.text: In 1666, Isaac Newton darkened his room, let sunlight through a hole, and split it into a rainbow with a glass prism.

buildup.text: People believed prisms added color to white light. Newton suspected the colors were already inside.

discovery.text: He used a second prism to recombine the rainbow back into white light—proving white light is a mixture of all colors.

twist.text: Newton was only 23 when he did this experiment during the plague, while Cambridge University was closed.

climax.text: This experiment founded the science of spectroscopy—now used to identify elements in distant stars.

punchline.text: White light isn't simple. It's every color combined. Newton proved it with glass.

quiz.question: How did Newton prove white light contains all colors?
options: He recombined the rainbow with a second prism back into white light
He mixed paints of every color together
He heated glass until it glowed white
